name: Chat: presence — "Active now" in thread header
desc:
The last remaining 1:1 gap vs the DoRent chat design: the thread header shows the counterpart's name but no "Active now" presence line.

Scope: a real presence service (SignalR connection tracking, last-seen), presence exposed on ChatConversationDetailsResponse, rendered in the thread header.

Do NOT fake it with a static label — the design implies live presence.

Unblocked: M-008 (docker nginx hub proxy) is FIXED as of 2026-07-11, so SignalR now works in the docker stack too.

Source: orchestrator gap analysis, 2026-07-11.

name: Chat: push notifications for new messages

No push/notification when a new chat message arrives while the app is closed or the user is not on the chat tab.

Realtime (SignalR) delivers in-app only. Consider reusing the existing Notification vertical (INotificationEmitter) and/or web push.

Source: knowledge/feature-notes/2026-07-chat-phase1.md — Deferred.

Phase 1 deferred "app-wide entry points (navbar message icon, home widget, mobile nav tab)". A chat link now exists in shared/ui/app-header, but coverage was never audited.

Scope: confirm which entry points exist vs the design (header icon w/ unread badge, home widget, mobile bottom-nav tab) and finish the missing ones.

Source: knowledge/feature-notes/2026-07-chat-phase1.md — Deferred.

name: Chat: open conversation with renter from owner-listing page

Rental-Ui/src/app/features/my-listings/pages/owner-listing-page/owner-listing-page.component.ts:248

name: Chat: mobile 375px — long toy title truncates mid-word
desc:
Cosmetic: on 375px the toy title in the thread's booking-context header truncates mid-word with an ellipsis.

Source: knowledge/feature-notes/2026-07-chat-phase1.md — Known follow-ups (from verifier, non-blocking).

name: Centralized API error handling & localization
desc:
Today every feature's NgRx effects pass HTTP errors straight to `toApiErrorMessage` (Rental-Ui/src/app/api/http-error-message.util.ts), which renders the server's `detail`/`title` VERBATIM. Two concrete consequences, both observed live by the verifier during the chat image-attachments work (2026-07-11):

1. On a 500, a full C# stack trace — including local filesystem paths — was rendered in the user-facing error banner.
2. Server-side rejections show hardcoded English. A text file renamed .jpg passes the browser's extension-derived File.type check, so the SERVER's magic-byte rejection is the one the user actually sees — and a Russian/Armenian user got English.

The chat feature now maps ServiceError codes to i18n keys, but ONLY locally in `features/chat/store/chat.effects.ts`. There is NO app-wide precedent: no other feature reads the server's error code at all. That local map is the seed for this task, not the solution.

Goal: one ErrorMapper for the whole app.
- map `error.code` → i18n key
- sanitize `detail` before it ever reaches the user
- never display internal exceptions or stack traces
- one consistent error banner/dialog surface
- a safe fallback for unknown/unmapped errors

RESOLVED since this card was written (2026-07-12): the ServiceError code no longer lives in the ProblemDetails `type` member. It moved to its own top-level **`errorCode`** member, `type` is now a `urn:rental:error:{code}` URI, and all 8 controllers go through one shared helper. The frontend contract layer models the envelope in `Rental-Ui/src/app/api/api-error.model.ts` (`ApiProblemDetails`, `ApiErrorCode` with the 52 real codes, and `getApiErrorCode()` — the single safe reader). USE `getApiErrorCode`; do not re-derive extraction, and never parse the `type` URN.

Source: verifier findings, chat image-attachments vertical (2026-07-11). See knowledge/mistakes.md M-013.
prompt:
Build centralized API error handling and localization in Rental-Ui (work on branch `dev`; do not commit, do not merge to main).

CONTEXT — read first:
- `Rental-Ui/src/app/api/api-error.model.ts` — the contract layer, ALREADY BUILT. `ApiProblemDetails` models the error envelope; `ApiErrorCode` enumerates the 52 real ServiceError codes; `getApiErrorCode()` is the single safe reader (the code lives in the top-level `errorCode` member; `type` is an opaque `urn:rental:error:{code}` URI — NEVER parse it). Use these; do not re-derive them.
- `Rental-Ui/src/app/api/http-error-message.util.ts` — today's behaviour and the core defect: renders the server's `detail`/`title` VERBATIM for any unmapped error. On a 500 this leaked a full C# stack trace with local filesystem paths into the user-facing banner (observed live by the verifier).
- `Rental-Ui/src/app/features/chat/store/chat.effects.ts` — the ONLY existing code→i18n mapping (`CHAT_ERROR_MESSAGE_KEYS`, 3 codes). Deliberately chat-local. This is your seed, not your solution.
- Every other feature's effects (auth, bookings, listings, reviews, admin, favorites, profile, notifications, public-profiles) is a bare passthrough — audit them all.

BUILD:
1. A shared ErrorMapper in `src/app/api/` — the single entry point every feature's effects use. It resolves the code via `getApiErrorCode`, maps it to an i18n key, and returns a translated message.
2. SANITIZE: `detail` must never reach the user raw. An unmapped error falls back to a safe, translated generic message, never to server-supplied prose, and NEVER to an exception message or stack trace. Treat this as a hard security rule, not a nicety.
3. Migrate every feature's effects to the shared mapper; delete the per-feature passthroughs and fold the chat-local map into the shared one.
4. One consistent error surface (banner/dialog) instead of each feature inventing its own.
5. i18n keys in en/ru/hy for every mapped code and for the fallback.

CONSTRAINTS:
- Do NOT change backend files.
- Codes come from `ApiErrorCode` / the backend's `ErrorCodes` constants — never invent code strings.

DONE WHEN: `npm run build` clean, `npm test` green, plus specs proving (a) a mapped code renders its translated message in a non-English locale, (b) an unmapped 500 whose `detail` contains a stack trace renders ONLY the generic fallback and leaks nothing. Report which features you migrated and any codes you could not map.

name: M-005: harden EF MigrateAsync against SQL-startup race (docker)
desc:
OPEN follow-up. The docker API crashes (exit 139) on restart: the db healthcheck (SELECT 1 on master) passes before the user DB is online, so EF Core Database.MigrateAsync() sees "does not exist", issues CREATE DATABASE, and hits SqlException 1801 (already exists) — unhandled, process dies.

Current workaround is operational: docker compose -f rental-api/docker-compose.yml up -d api (succeeds on retry).

Proper fix: retry loop / EnableRetryOnFailure in MigrationExtensions.ApplyMigrationsAsync so transient SQL-startup errors are retried instead of crashing.

Source: knowledge/mistakes.md — M-005 (still OPEN).

name: Content-Security-Policy header — deferred to a later sprint
desc:
rental-api/src/RentalPlatform.Api/Middleware/SecurityHeadersMiddleware.cs:4
"// it requires per-route asset allowlists and is deferred to a later sprint."

CSP is not emitted. Scope: define per-route asset allowlists and enable the header.

name: rental-api/README.md is outdated — rewrite or delete stale sections
desc:
M-001. The README describes an early toys-MVP: no reviews, no notifications, no booking activate/complete/cancel, no listing Draft/Archived statuses, wrong Angular version. CLAUDE.md already warns agents not to trust it.

Source of truth is controllers + api-contract.ts + domain enums. Bring the README in line or delete the stale sections.

Source: knowledge/mistakes.md — M-001.

name: Backups: off-site copy of production backups
desc:
Daily backups (.bak + uploads tgz, cron 03:30, 7-day retention) land ONLY in /opt/dorent/backups on the same VPS — a single disk failure or Hetzner account loss takes both the database and its backups. Decision needed from Tigran first: Backblaze B2 free tier (10 GB, via rclone) vs periodic pull to his PC.

Source: production deploy session 2026-07-13; DEPLOY-PRODUCTION.md backup section marks off-site as pending; ADR-003 amendment.
prompt: Prerequisite from Tigran: a Backblaze B2 account + application key (or an explicit choice of the pull-to-PC variant). Then, on the dorent.am server (SSH): install rclone, configure a B2 remote (credentials live only on the server, never in the repo), extend deploy/backup-production.sh (rental-api repo, branch dev) with an rclone copy of /opt/dorent/backups after a successful local backup, add remote-side retention (e.g. 30 days), update the backup section of DEPLOY-PRODUCTION.md, and prove it: run the cron entry once manually, then restore a .bak downloaded FROM B2 via ./deploy/backup-production.sh --verify semantics. Commit script/doc changes on dev, do not push to main.

name: Monitoring: UptimeRobot for dorent.am
desc:
No uptime monitoring: if the site goes down, nobody is notified. Human task (needs a personal account, no agent access): sign up at uptimerobot.com (free plan), add HTTP(s) monitor for https://dorent.am/ (5-min interval, email alert), plus a Keyword monitor on https://dorent.am/api/listings?page=1&pageSize=1 (keyword: items) to catch the nginx-alive-but-API-dead case.

Source: production deploy session 2026-07-13, stage 7.

name: Security: change bootstrap admin password + rotate Cloudflare tunnel token
desc:
Two hygiene items from the 2026-07-13 production deploy:

1. The bootstrap admin password (login tigransargsyan062@gmail.com) was machine-generated at deploy time and is stored outside the server. Log in at https://dorent.am and change it via profile — after that the stored copy becomes a dead artifact.

2. The Cloudflare tunnel token transited the chat once during setup. Rotate it: Cloudflare Zero Trust → Networks → Tunnels → dorent-production → Refresh token, put the new value into /opt/dorent/rental-api/.env (CLOUDFLARE_TUNNEL_TOKEN), then docker compose -f docker-compose.production.yml up -d cloudflared.

Mostly human steps; an agent can do item 2 over SSH once the new token is provided in .env.

name: DNS: clean up legacy cPanel records in Cloudflare zone dorent.am
desc:
The zone still carries the dead Name.am cPanel legacy, all pointing at the suspended host 161.97.154.98: A-records cpanel/cpcalendars/cpcontacts/ftp/webdisk/webmail/whm, CNAME mail → dorent.am, a Cloudflare placeholder MX (_dc-mx.…), SPF TXT referencing the dead IP, and cPanel SRV/DKIM/ACME/DCV records. None of them serve anything.

Constraints agreed during the deploy: mail @dorent.am is unused today but may be wanted later; nothing gets deleted without Tigran's explicit per-record approval. The live records dorent.am + www.dorent.am (type Tunnel → dorent-production) must not be touched.
prompt: Ask Tigran for a fresh screenshot/export of Cloudflare DNS → Records for dorent.am. Produce a two-column table: KEEP (the two Tunnel records, and anything Tigran wants for future mail) vs DELETE (every record pointing at 161.97.154.98 or cPanel services: A cpanel/cpcalendars/cpcontacts/ftp/webdisk/webmail/whm, CNAME mail, placeholder MX _dc-mx.*, SPF TXT with ip4:161.97.154.98, SRV _autodiscover/_caldav*/_carddav*, cPanel DKIM/ACME/DCV TXT). Present the table, get explicit approval, then walk Tigran through deleting (or delete via API if credentials are provided). Note in the table: when real mail is wanted later, proper MX/SPF/DKIM for the chosen provider (e.g. Zoho/Google) replace the legacy ones — the tunnel records are unrelated.

name: Auth: register https://dorent.am in Google OAuth origins
desc:
Google Sign-In on production will fail until https://dorent.am (and https://www.dorent.am) are added to Authorized JavaScript origins of the OAuth client ending …oupt.apps.googleusercontent.com in Google Cloud Console. Human step (console access); afterwards verify the Google login flow live on https://dorent.am.

Source: production deploy session 2026-07-13, open item.

name: Infra: rescale VPS to 4 GB (CPX21) when budget allows
desc:
The 2 GB CPX12 + 4 GB swap + MSSQL_MEMORY_LIMIT_MB=1024 setup is a deliberate temporary compromise (ADR-003, amended 2026-07-13) — under real traffic SQL Server will start swapping and latency will degrade.

Upgrade path with minimal risk: Hetzner console → server → Rescale → CPX21 (3 vCPU / 4 GB) with "CPU and RAM only" (keeps the 40 GB disk, so the change stays reversible), ~2 min downtime. Afterwards raise or drop MSSQL_MEMORY_LIMIT_MB in docker-compose.production.yml (rental-api dev branch) and consider removing the 4 GB swap dependency note in DEPLOY-PRODUCTION.md.

name: QA: accessibility automation baseline
desc:
Deliberately deferred out of the qa-engineer agent scope (2026-07-14): a11y automation without a baseline just automates noise. Prerequisite: a one-time manual a11y audit of the critical pages (axe DevTools or similar) to establish the baseline; then decide what is worth automating (axe-core in the mocked Playwright tier is the likely cheapest layer).

Source: qa-engineer design discussion, 2026-07-14.

name: QA: cross-browser matrix for critical journeys
desc:
Deliberately deferred out of the qa-engineer agent scope (2026-07-14): Playwright currently installs chromium only. After the real-stack booking journey suite stabilizes, evaluate adding firefox/webkit projects for the mocked (fast) tier only — real-stack stays single-browser to keep runtime sane.

Source: qa-engineer design discussion, 2026-07-14.

name: Infra: stop merging console output into backup.log (cron redirect)
desc:
Design smell behind M-015: the crontab entry appends the backup script's stdout+stderr into the same file the script writes structured entries to:

  30 3 * * * /opt/dorent/rental-api/deploy/backup-production.sh >> /opt/dorent/backups/backup.log 2>&1

That interleaving is what made smoke.sh grade prose instead of the outcome (fixed defensively in rental-api 33618a5, but the smell remains). Cleaner end state: drop the >> redirect entirely (the script already logs its own outcomes and its ERR trap records failures), or send console output to a separate /opt/dorent/backups/backup.out.

Server-side change (crontab) + DEPLOY-PRODUCTION.md update. Task for platform-engineer.

Source: knowledge/mistakes.md M-015, 2026-07-14.

name: Repo hygiene: .gitattributes eol=lf for deploy/*.sh + fix stale git remote URL
desc:
Two small papercuts found during the 2026-07-14 production work (platform-engineer):

1. deploy/*.sh are stored LF in the git index but check out CRLF on Windows. Harmless for git, but a script scp'd straight from a Windows working tree to Linux gets a broken shebang (env: 'bash'). Fix: add .gitattributes with `*.sh text eol=lf` in rental-api (and consider the same for Rental-Ui).

2. `git push` in rental-api prints "remote: This repository moved" — origin still points at github.com/Tigran-developer/rental-api while the repo now lives at TTigSSar/rental-api. GitHub redirects today, but redirects are not forever. Fix: `git remote set-url origin https://github.com/TTigSSar/rental-api.git` (same check for Rental-Ui).

name: Harden dormant GetValue<bool> trap in ForwardedHeadersExtensions (M-016 twin)
desc:
Dormant instance of the M-016 class, found by backend-dev while fixing the demo-content flag (2026-07-16):

`ForwardedHeadersExtensions.cs:23` uses `section.GetValue("Enabled", false)` — the default-value overload applies only when the key is fully ABSENT, not when it is present-but-empty. An empty string still throws InvalidOperationException at startup.

Currently unreachable: `ForwardedHeaders__Enabled` is not mapped in docker-compose.production.yml, so no env var can feed it an empty string. It becomes a live outage the moment anyone wires it (compose renders unset vars as "", and a startup throw = full site outage, since ui/cloudflared gate on api service_healthy).

Fix: mirror the tolerant parse used in DemoContentBootstrapExtensions.ParseDemoContentEnabled (bool.TryParse → warn + default, never throw). See knowledge/mistakes.md M-016.

Also check ForwardLimit (currently GetValue<int?> — nullable, safe) and any future value-type config reads on the startup path.

name: A11y: --ui-focus-ring fails WCAG 2.2 SC 1.4.11 app-wide
desc:
Surfaced by frontend-dev while polishing the chat inbox to the approved design (2026-07-16, commit d49fe79 on Rental-Ui dev).

`src/styles.css:121` defines `--ui-focus-ring: 0 0 0 3px rgba(255, 96, 8, 0.18)` — a soft orange halo at 18% alpha. WCAG 2.2 SC 1.4.11 (Non-text Contrast) requires >= 3:1 for a focus indicator. Measured against real surfaces:

- white surface: 1.23:1 — fail
- mobile cream tint (#FAF8F4): 1.22:1 — fail
- chat active row (--ui-color-primary-soft): 1.08:1 — effectively invisible

The halo paints correctly — the CSS is not broken. It simply carries no contrast. Bordered controls (buttons, the composer, View profile) LOOK acceptable only because the halo hugs an existing border and the border does the visual work; the indicator itself is still non-conformant. Any borderless/transparent element gets no perceivable focus at all — which is exactly how this was found: chat inbox rows were pixel-identical focused vs unfocused, and a focused ACTIVE row was indistinguishable from an unfocused one, so a keyboard user could not tell "this is open" from "this is where my focus is".

Scope: 26 usages across 8 files (`grep -rn "var(--ui-focus-ring)" src --include=*.css --include=*.scss`).

Already fixed LOCALLY in chat only (conversations-page.component.scss ~L242/L255): a solid inset ring on --ui-color-primary-strong layered under the halo — 3.32-3.77:1, passes. Chat is currently the ONLY place in the app with a conformant focus indicator; that inconsistency is the real reason to do this.

Deferred deliberately, not forgotten: fixing the token is an app-wide visual change touching screens outside the chat work, and the approved design defines no focus states, so there is no source of truth to defer to — it needs a human call.
prompt:
Fix the app-wide keyboard focus indicator in Rental-Ui so it meets WCAG 2.2 SC 1.4.11 (>= 3:1 contrast for non-text indicators). Work on branch `dev` (nested repo; the human merges to main).

PROBLEM
`src/styles.css:121`: `--ui-focus-ring: 0 0 0 3px rgba(255, 96, 8, 0.18)`. At 18% alpha it measures 1.23:1 on white, 1.22:1 on the cream tint, 1.08:1 on --ui-color-primary-soft. It paints but is not perceivable. Bordered controls only look fine because the halo hugs their border; borderless elements show no focus at all.

REFERENCE FIX (already shipped, chat only)
`src/app/features/chat/pages/conversations-page/conversations-page.component.scss` ~L242 and ~L255 layer a solid inset ring under the halo:
  box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 2px var(--ui-color-primary-strong), var(--ui-focus-ring);
--ui-color-primary-strong (#e5530a) measures 3.77:1 white / 3.55:1 cream / 3.32:1 on the soft fill. --ui-color-primary (#ff6008) was tried and FAILS two of the three (3.03/2.86/2.67) — do not use it.

TASK
1. Decide the approach and propose it BEFORE mass-editing: either redefine --ui-focus-ring itself to a conformant solid ring, or add a companion token (e.g. --ui-focus-ring-solid) and migrate usages. Redefining is cleaner but shifts the look of all 26 usages at once; a companion token allows an incremental sweep. State the trade-off and get a human decision.
2. Enumerate the blast radius: `grep -rn "var(--ui-focus-ring)" src --include=*.css --include=*.scss` — 26 usages in 8 files at time of writing.
3. Apply, keeping element box sizes stable (prefer inset rings; outer rings get clipped inside overflow containers — the chat rail hit exactly this).
4. Once done, collapse the chat-local override in conversations-page.component.scss if the global token now covers it, so the special case does not linger.
5. Verify each of the 8 files visually by tabbing through with the KEYBOARD, at 390px and 1280px.

TRAPS (both cost real time when this was first investigated — do not rediscover them)
- Clicking an element first poisons :focus-visible for later programmatic .focus() calls (Chrome last-interaction heuristic) and fabricates a false "no ring" reading. Drive the repro by keyboard only.
- Reading computed box-shadow immediately after focus returns the TRANSITION START value (rgba(0,0,0,0) 0px 0px 0px 0px) because .chat-row has `transition: box-shadow 0.15s`. Wait for the transition or read after it settles.

CONSTRAINTS
- The repo is entirely CRLF. Verify formatting with `npx prettier --check --end-of-line crlf <files>`. NEVER run `prettier --write` without --end-of-line crlf — it rewrites line endings repo-wide.
- The approved design defines no focus states; this is an accessibility decision, not a design-fidelity one.

DONE WHEN: `npx ng build` clean, `npx ng test --watch=false` green (baseline 29 files / 264 tests), every focusable control shows a >= 3:1 indicator, and screenshots prove focus is visible on both bordered and borderless controls.

name: Listing wizard: Pickup area — district picker vs free-text city/address
desc:
Deferred from the create-listing design-alignment pass (2026-07-17). The DoRent design (design project Listing.html, listing-desktop.jsx:160-164 / flow-a-mvp-final.jsx:139-147) renders Pickup area as a SINGLE district select — pin icon + "Kentron, Yerevan" + chevron — selecting from a known district list.

The code instead has TWO free-text inputs: city (required) + addressLine (optional) — create-listing-form.component.html:576-600.

Human decision (Tigran, 2026-07-17): keep the current two-field form for now; the district picker needs a canonical Yerevan districts data source and reshapes what city/addressLine mean on the payload, which is more than a cosmetic pass.

Note: the privacy-note copy already matches the design exactly, but the code attaches it to the optional addressLine while the design attaches it to the required district select.
prompt:
Replace the create-listing wizard's free-text City + Pickup-area inputs with a single Yerevan district picker, matching the DoRent design (Pickup area = one select: pin icon + district + chevron). Work on branch dev.

DESIGN: design project Listing.html — listing-desktop.jsx:160-164 (desktop), flow-a-mvp-final.jsx:139-147 (mobile). One required field labelled "Pickup area", value like "Kentron, Yerevan", chevron-down, privacy note "We only show your district to renters — never your full address." attached beneath it.

CURRENT STATE: two app-ui-inputs in Rental-Ui/src/app/features/listings/components/create-listing-form/create-listing-form.component.html:576-600 — city (required, form control) and addressLine (optional). Payload built in onSubmit() in create-listing-form.component.ts.

DECISIONS TO GET FROM THE HUMAN BEFORE CODING:
1. Districts data source — seeded constant in the frontend, or a backend endpoint (e.g. GET /api/geo/districts)? Yerevan has ~12 administrative districts; if the marketplace expands beyond Yerevan this must generalize.
2. Payload mapping — does the chosen district populate city, addressLine, or a new field? City is [Required] on the backend DTO today; dropping free-text city changes what that column holds.
3. Existing listings with free-text city values — migration/backfill, or leave as-is and only constrain new listings?

This is a SYSTEM change (data source + contract + possibly migration), not a restyle — plan it in plan mode and get the plan approved. contract-guardian must sync any DTO change; the migration (if any) gets human review.

name: Listing wizard: header "Save" / draft persistence
desc:
Deferred from the create-listing design-alignment pass (2026-07-17). The design's shared wizard header (flow-a-mvp-final.jsx:21) renders a "Save" button at the header's right edge on every step; the code renders an empty spacer there (create-listing-form.component.html:26).

Evidence this was specced and abandoned: Rental-Ui/public/i18n/en.json carries orphan keys listings.createForm.draft.resumeTitle / draft.resume / draft.discard with NO consumer anywhere in features/listings.

Human decision (Tigran, 2026-07-17): defer. "Save" implies draft persistence — a listing-draft store/endpoint and a resume-on-return flow — which is a feature, not a wizard restyle. ListingStatus already has a Draft(0) state, so the domain concept exists; what's missing is the create-flow wiring.
prompt:
Add draft save/resume to the create-listing wizard so an owner can leave mid-flow and come back. Work on branch dev. This is a feature — plan in plan mode and get the plan approved before coding.

CONTEXT: the design shows a "Save" button in the wizard header on every step (flow-a-mvp-final.jsx:21 in the design project). Orphan i18n keys already exist unused in Rental-Ui/public/i18n/*.json: listings.createForm.draft.resumeTitle, draft.resume, draft.discard. ListingStatus.Draft(0) already exists in the domain (see rental-api status machine in CLAUDE.md).

DECISIONS TO GET FROM THE HUMAN FIRST:
1. Where drafts live — reuse Listing rows in Draft status (photos are the complication: they upload to the server, so a draft with photos means real ListingImage rows), or a separate lightweight draft store / localStorage for an unsubmitted wizard?
2. Trigger — explicit "Save" button only, or also autosave on step change?
3. Resume UX — a "resume your draft" prompt on returning to /list, or a drafts section under my-listings?

Surfaces: create-listing-form.component.{ts,html} (the header spacer at html:26 becomes the Save button), the create-listing page/store, and backend draft endpoints if server-persisted. contract-guardian syncs any DTO/route change; any migration gets human review.

name: Listing wizard: preview "Verified" / "Hygiene checked" badges
desc:
Deferred from the create-listing design-alignment pass (2026-07-17). The design's Step-5 preview card shows two trust badges above the title — "Verified" (success tone) and "Hygiene checked" (info tone): flow-a-mvp-final.jsx:267-270, listing-desktop.jsx:223-226. The code renders neither.

Human decision (Tigran, 2026-07-17): defer, and do NOT render "Verified" naively. Two different data problems:
- "Hygiene checked" is trivially derivable from the Step-4 cleaning checklist (cleanWashed/cleanDisinfected/cleanUV signals already in the component).
- "Verified" reflects owner/listing verification state the create form has NO access to. Rendering a hard-coded "Verified" badge on a listing that hasn't even been submitted for review would be a FALSE trust signal shown to the owner (and worse if it leaked to the public card). Needs a real verification data source + a product definition of what "Verified" means and when it's earned.
prompt:
Add the two trust badges from the DoRent design to listings, honestly. Work on branch dev. Plan in plan mode; get approval.

DESIGN: Step-5 preview card, badges above the title — "Verified" (success) + "Hygiene checked" (info): flow-a-mvp-final.jsx:267-270, listing-desktop.jsx:223-226 in the design project.

DO IT IN TWO SEPARABLE PIECES — they have very different risk:
1. "Hygiene checked" — LOW risk, derive it from the Step-4 cleaning checklist already in create-listing-form.component.ts (cleanWashed/cleanDisinfected/cleanUV). Decide the threshold (any step? all three?) with the human. This is close to cosmetic.
2. "Verified" — DO NOT render from the create form. This badge asserts a trust fact the form cannot know. Get a product definition first: what makes a listing/owner "Verified" (ID check? admin approval? owner-verification flow that may not exist yet)? Where is that state stored? Only render the badge where that data is actually available (likely the public listing card / details page, not the pre-submit preview). Rendering it speculatively is worse than omitting it.

Whichever piece is approved: contract-guardian syncs any DTO change; keep the badge out of any surface where the underlying fact isn't real.

name: Listing wizard: rate-unit "typical price" hints need real market data
desc:
Deferred from the create-listing design-alignment pass (2026-07-17). The design's rate-unit dropdown (pricing-rates.jsx:6-12,77-81) shows a second line per unit — "Typical <range>" pricing guidance — to help owners price their toy.

During the alignment pass a frontend agent ported these by converting the design's MOCK ruble figures to USD by arithmetic (~90 RUB/USD). Tigran (2026-07-17) had them REMOVED before merge: owner-facing pricing guidance invented from mock data would be believed and acted on — an owner would set a wrong price trusting a number we made up. Omitting beats inventing.

This card is to do it PROPERLY: derive real typical-price ranges per rental unit (hourly/daily/weekly/monthly/yearly) from actual Armenian/Yerevan child-toy rental market data (or from the platform's own listing price distribution once there's enough data), then re-add the hint line. Until real numbers exist, the dropdown correctly shows single-line rows with no hint.
prompt:
Re-add the "typical price" guidance line to the create-listing rate-unit dropdown, using REAL data — not invented figures. Work on branch dev.

BACKGROUND: the DoRent design (pricing-rates.jsx:6-12,77-81) shows each rental-unit row with a "Typical <low>-<high>" second line. A prior pass added these by converting mock rubles to USD arithmetically; they were removed before merge because fabricated pricing guidance is acted on by owners and does real harm. The dropdown currently renders single-line rows (badge + label + check) in create-listing-form.component.html / .menu.scss, with the hint method and i18n keys deleted.

TASK:
1. Source real typical-price ranges per unit (Hourly/Daily/Weekly/Monthly/Yearly) in the platform's display currency (USD today — see CURRENCY_CODE in create-listing-form.component.ts). Options: market research on Armenian toy-rental pricing, OR compute from the platform's own approved-listing price distribution per unit once sample size is adequate (preferred long-term — self-calibrating). Get the human to confirm the source and the numbers.
2. Re-add the hint line: an i18n key per unit in ALL locale files (en/ru/hy), rendered as the dropdown row's second line. Restore the two-line row layout in .menu.scss (there was a .rate-card__menu-text / .rate-card__menu-hint pair; .rate-card__menu-label had flex:1 when single-line).
3. If computed from platform data, decide refresh cadence and where the computation lives (backend endpoint vs build-time constant).

CONSTRAINT: never ship a made-up number here. If real data isn't available, this card stays open — the single-line dropdown is the correct interim state.

name: Design source: Step 2 create-listing mockup still carries stale 4-step chrome
desc:
Found during the create-listing design-alignment pass (2026-07-17). This is a bug in the DESIGN PROJECT, not the code.

The DoRent "List your toy" flow is a 5-step wizard (Photos, Basics, Pricing & Location, Safety & Hygiene, Preview & Submit). Four of the five mobile steps use the shared 5-step FAWizardHeader/FAWizardFooter. But Step 2 (FACreateListing, flow-a-extra.jsx:9) was never migrated — it still renders its own inline header reading "Step 2 of 4 · Basics" with a 4-SEGMENT progress bar (flow-a-extra.jsx:19,22-26) and its own inline Back/Continue row.

The implementation is CORRECT (uniform 5-step chrome on every step); the design is internally inconsistent and stale on this one screen. No code change — this is a note to fix the mockup upstream in the Claude Design project (df24299b-1299-4804-8a0a-5484005594c3) so the design stops contradicting itself and the shipped app. Recorded so the next person diffing design-vs-code doesn't re-report the code as wrong.
prompt: Fix the DoRent design mockup (Claude Design project df24299b-1299-4804-8a0a-5484005594c3, file Listing.html), NOT the app code. Migrate the mobile Step-2 screen FACreateListing (flow-a-extra.jsx:9) off its stale inline 4-step chrome onto the shared 5-step FAWizardHeader/FAWizardFooter that the other four steps (flow-a-mvp-final.jsx) already use. It currently renders "Step 2 of 4" with a 4-segment progress bar and its own inline Back/Continue row, contradicting the 5-step flow and the shipped app. Use the DesignSync tool + /design-sync skill; edit incrementally, one component. Do not touch the Angular app — the code is already correct.
